reviews.review_title: GREAT APP but Quest now not booting it
reviews.review_description:
Ignore the complaints here about images from users who don’t follow the simple phone app scanning recommendations or do not know how to navigate within a mispositioned world. 
This is NOT simply a “travel app” it’s a vr display of their phone social scanning app that creates WALKABLE VR scans of your surroundings and share them with the world. (And no, I haven’t even seen a scan of someone’s meal) 
The scans are GREAT when done properly, and the Quest app has an option to explore the highly rated scans. 
No,the phone app is not as thorough as using Meta Hyperscape on your Quest, but you can scan with only your phone (without looking like a headset dork in public) and the scans are rendered in your phone relatively fast, (without waiting for hours on a cloud render). They can be kept private in your phone, or easily shared with friends or the whole world by uploading it to the map app (including those without a vr headset). 

BUT, SINCE YESTERDAY, THE QUEST VIEWING APP WONT LOAD. WHAT GIVES?!?!?

Absolutely nothing renders properly; everything looks like it's shrouded in thick fog. I don't understand how anyone could release an app in such an unusable state. It's early access, so I'm sure it will improve. Today, however, it's the clearest example of a 1-star app that I've ever seen.

This is an app that attempts to connect you w/ other people & their experiences but it’s just a bunch of crappy pictures of food. Congratulations, you’ve invented a quasi-3D Yelp. 
Great concept until you realize nobody using this really has anything meaningful going on …Just a bunch of take-out bills.

Scaniverse has become my new obsession. Why just take a 2D photo of something when you can do a reality capture of the scene or object and view it later in full 3D? Traveling around the world viewing places through other's eyes is fantastic. Even though some scans are less than perfect this is kind of their charm as they turn sometimes into impressionistic dream like encounters that I find enjoyable.  

I'm surprised there are so many negative reviews here as when captured properly your scans can be amazing. There is a slight learning curve though and I suspect too may people don't read the scanning hints and just haphazardly point and shoot. Reading the instructions, always a good idea. Also, Niantic continues to improve the app since it's inception so disappointed users should give it another try.
Locations are poor quality thus experience is not immersive

The quality is very bad or it gets worse as we move and we don't really have a 360° either. Some scans are just black or... selfies? I don't know if there is a validation process. Also, I don't understand the concept, there are apps for visiting places in the world. How can this one make the difference?

reviews.review_title: Fantastic to see Gaussian splats in vr
reviews.review_description: It’s so cool to see your own splats on the quest 3 natively. Obviously there’s some technical limitations but the cleaner scans really do look impressive. One issue is splat unresponsive for certain areas that aren’t optimized for Vr (such as your own splats occasionally). You can get around this by using higher cpu/gpu allocation in quest games optimizer (available on itch..io) or Niantic could change their app settings in future updates. Hopefully some optimizations to come as well. Very exciting stuff

reviews.review_title: From my phone to VR in just minutes!
reviews.review_description:
It is so satisfying to visit the splats I made on my mobile phone in VR in just minutes.

I've been building a library of 3D scans and 3D photos of the places I visited this year. When I connected my Scaniverse account to Into The Scaniverse, I could see my full library of scans in VR, like magic.

It shows some promise, and you can't really complain too much about free stuff, but there are far better sightseeing apps out there. What I really don't get about this app is why the specific subject matter is in focus, but the whole of the rest of the image is a blurred mess. That has obviously been done deliberately, and taken some time to achieve, but I cannot for the life of me understand why they would have done that.

Making gaussian splat scans has become my new favourite hobby since I discovered Scaniverse through launch of this VR companion app. It’s fascinating to see nearly photorealistic renderings of places around my neighborhood, then being able to virtually fly above, in, or around those places.

The VR app could benefit from some missing features present in the phone app:
-Selecting a person’s username to see a gallery of their scans
-Re-centering your view to the initial starting position
-Selecting a portion of the scan to quickly zoom in on it, the way double tapping works on the phone. The joystick movement speed is too slow when viewing large scans.

It would be nice to have the option to select a higher level of detail at the cost of performance. The VR versions of scans are noticeably less detailed than when doing the same scans on the phone app. I’d also like an option for smooth turning rather than only snap turning.

There are also some bugs. When viewing the My Splats section, the order of thumbnails is sometimes randomized when changing pages, instead of showing in order of upload date. It would help to have a refresh button on the My Splats section to avoid this issue. That would also help to avoid having to reload the entire app if I upload a new scan from my phone while the VR app is still running, as it seems that section doesn’t repopulate automatically, but rather only when you initially load the app.

These missing features and bugs are what is preventing me from giving it a five star review.

Man it’s been quite a while since a VR app actually made me feel violently ill but this one got the job done. The environments here look like they’ve been rammed through an AI filter until they were barely recognizable. Niantic should stick to their roots and focus on their mobile games cause they clearly ain’t capable of developing a decent VR app.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for sharing your experience with Into the Scaniverse. I'm sorry to hear that you had a difficult experience with the app - user comfort is very important to us.

I understand your concerns about the visual quality of the environments. Since Into the Scaniverse features user-generated 3D scans, the quality can vary significantly. This community-driven approach means you'll encounter a range of scenes with different levels of detail.

Your feedback helps us improve the app experience. Our team is constantly working to enhance the technology that renders these 3D scans to make them more comfortable to view and explore.

If you'd be willing to try again, you might find better experiences by exploring featured content which typically has higher quality scans. We also welcome any specific suggestions you might have for improving the visual quality or reducing motion discomfort. You can share suggestions on our community forum: https://community.scaniverse.com/c/its/22

reviews.review_title: Great but I cant upload from Android
reviews.review_description: Very cool piece of software but I haven't been able to upload my scans to the map or share online from the Android app. The share link option ends up with "creating link failed" error. And if I try to upload to the map it attempts to upload and eventually gives an error message of "no internet" or similar wording. My phone is Galaxy S20 and connected to wifi on a fast connection with no connection issues (connection works for any other internet access on the phone). Have tried over the last few hours. I'm in Australia if that makes any difference. Wondering if there is a timeout issue if uploads to your server are too saturated during a peak time?

This is very disappointing, every scan I tried was a blurry ugly mess with almost no detail. The hot air balloon looks like PS1 graphics made by a developer. It all feels super amateurish and rushed. Waste of time and headset storage.

This app can only get better the more people upload.

Unfortunately, my first upload only appears in my profile. It's not showing on the public map & I haven't found any way to get it to do so. Hopefully it can be worked out.

SirRealDeal's Cockpit if it ever shows up. Check it out.

I love this. Only done a couple of scans, but it's great to immerse yourself in them. I also enjoy visiting other folks' scans.

I'd love to have an optional feature that records the soundscape around your object for true immersion; that would be fantastic.
This is a great way to explore the world and see what others have posted from Scaniverse, but you can also look at your own private scans in a headset.

I’m using Meta’s Hyperscape to perform 3D scans, but I don’t like the fact that they are streamed and can’t be downloaded.

Your app came to mind: why don’t you create your own data processing system to generate 3D environments from scans that users would send you through their headsets?

It would work exactly like Hyperscape: you would receive the data, generate the 3D environment, and then allow the user to download the processed content (for a fee).
Each scan — and its corresponding processing on your servers — could have a cost, or users could purchase credit packages for a certain number of scans.

The most important aspect, however, is that users should be able to download their own scans for offline viewing.
This would greatly increase usability, considering that it’s not always easy to have a stable Wi-Fi connection, and even less a high-speed one — especially if someone wants to travel and show their creations to other people.

--------





Developer Idea: Create a rating system for scans so that users can see the overall rating and the number of votes for each scan, giving them an idea of the scan's quality before even opening it. Thanks.

It’s great that you can easily view your 3D scans in VR and explore a wide variety of community-created ones. Naturally, you'll come across some low-quality scans - that's to be expected - but it would be nice to have better filtering and searching options to sort through them. The overall functionality is fairly limited, and there’s definitely a lot of room for improvement. Giving my experience I would give it less stars but I see that the devs are currently working on it so I want to keep them motivated. Finally the app is free, so it’s just worth checking out.

I’ve been using Scaniverse for years now on iPhone but now I can finally see my creations as they meant to be seen. Granted, it’s not perfect, figuring out how to actually share my models took some trial and error (not straightforward, but doable). Hopefully Scaniverse capture get better in the future until it’s super realistic.

It is astonishingly great to be able to scan a scene or object with your phone, and then to be able to view it in VR minutes later.  After linking your scaniverse account you can upload your scans to be either public or unlisted.   The scanning process is easy, just walk around the subject! Your phone does the processing and after uploading, you use this app to view them in VR.  The rendered 3d scenes are pretty amazing to view in VR, they are very realistic. You can walk around a whole scan that you made.  This app also allows you to view other peoples scans so its great to travel virtually.   All around excellent app!

I see the developer tried a Google Earth
 knockoff but such bad imagery.

reviews.review_title: Early Access - pretty cool so far!
reviews.review_description: I love the idea. I started to play around on my android phone. I checked many scans. Great! After I installed the app on my Quest3, I can't see the same scans in VR as on Android? There are just a handfull available in VR? Both "worlds" not synced yet?

reviews.review_title: Finally a way to view my scans in VR
reviews.review_description: So cool that I can see so many other scans people have done. Also no more exporting of files and trying to find a compatible viewer

The visual quality of the scans is incredibly bad, at least on Quest 3. Maybe Gaussian splatting isn’t ready for VR where one can see it up close and where filling the large field of view with high quality just requires too many particles.

I have installed it, the app starts, but I keep getting a 'Locations server error: unable to connect to the server, please try again later'. I have tried multiple times and keep getting this, I am not able to view any of the scans.

developer_response.body: Hi there, sorry that you're having issues accessing Into The Scaniverse! We recommend double-checking that you have an active internet connection when launching the app, and giving it another shot. Otherwise, please reach out to us for support in our forums and we'll do our best to fix the issue! https://community.scaniverse.com/c/its/22

reviews.review_description: Does not work as advertised. Phone app and quest app do not integrate well. every time you open the app, it resets to a different view. ￼
I think the thing this title is missing, even though the tech is cool, is community. It’s not exciting for more than 5 minutes by yourself
A good software
Gaussian Splatting 😃
